answer:
The curves intersect when \((\sin x + \cos x)^4 = (\sin x - \cos x)^4\), which simplifies to \(|\sin x + \cos x| = |\sin x - \cos x|\), giving \(\cos x = 0\) or \(\sin x = 0\).  
The smallest positive intersection after \(x=0\) is at \(x = \pi/2\), so \(a = \pi/2\).  
The area between the curves from \(0\) to \(\pi/2\) is \(\int_0^{\pi/2} \left[(\sin x + \cos x)^4 - (\sin x - \cos x)^4\right] dx = 2\).

\boxed{C}
